task_completion.description: Log in to Splunk web interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Splunk web interface is visible, either at the login screen with credentials entered or the logged-in home screen.

task_completion.description: Query the correct index for web access logs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Splunk search bar contains a query starting with 'index=tutorial' or 'index=web_logs'.

task_completion.description: Aggregate the average and maximum lengths of the requested URI per client IP.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The search query contains functions to calculate string length (e.g., eval len=len(uri)) and aggregate them (e.g., stats avg(len) max(len) by clientip).

task_completion.description: Filter the results where the average URI length is strictly greater than 60.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The search query contains a filtering clause like 'where avg_len > 60' or 'search avg > 60' applied after the aggregation.

task_completion.description: Save the search query as a Report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Save As Report' dialog box is open, or a success message confirms the report has been created.
The text 'URI_Length_Anomaly_Hunt' is visible in the Title field of the 'Save As Report' dialog, or in the Splunk Reports list.

integrity.description: The agent used the Splunk Web UI in a browser rather than terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: A web browser window showing the Splunk interface at localhost:8000.

integrity.description: The agent actually executed the search and retrieved results.
integrity.visual_evidence: Search results (events or statistics table) are visible below the search bar in the Splunk UI.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply write the expected answers into a text file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Interactions occur within the Splunk application, not in a text editor or terminal echoing the report name.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The required report name is exactly 'URI_Length_Anomaly_Hunt'. The Splunk credentials are 'admin' and 'SplunkAdmin1!'. The search must target the 'tutorial' or 'web_logs' index, calculate the length of the URI, use 'stats' to find the average and maximum length by client IP, and filter for average length > 60.
